decision: Should the team pilot the fictional AI procurement copilot in supplier onboarding?
decision_body: Risk and Compliance Council, chaired by the Head of Procurement Operations.
default_outcome: Do not pilot; retain the existing manual review workflow.
why_now: Document rework is delaying onboarding, while the next council review provides a bounded decision point.

claims.claim: The synthetic baseline records 18% rework on document completeness.
claims.weakest_link: The mixed document classes may hide variation.
claims.gap_that_changes_mind: A clean re-count below 8% would weaken the pilot case.

claims.claim: A retrieval prototype answered 70% of a synthetic 50-case set correctly.
claims.gap_that_changes_mind: Accuracy below 50% on an unseen set would stop the pilot.

claims.claim: Human approval is required before any supplier-facing action.
claims.weakest_link: Approval and full review are not yet distinguished.
claims.gap_that_changes_mind: An unresolved review definition would make acceptance criteria impossible.

claims.claim: Risk review should cover context, impacts, measurement, and treatment rather than model accuracy alone.
claims.weakest_link: The framework does not prescribe this local workflow.
claims.gap_that_changes_mind: If the council cannot map the pilot to named controls, postpone it.

claims.claim: The pilot must test sensitive-information disclosure and excessive agency risks.
claims.weakest_link: The taxonomy identifies risks but does not prove control effectiveness.
claims.gap_that_changes_mind: A critical disclosure or unauthorized action in testing would block the pilot.

uncertainties: The unseen-case accuracy distribution is unknown.
The cost of complete audit logging is not yet measured.
The effect of reviewer learning and behavior change over the pilot is unknown.

artifact.form: Public fictional case plus internal pilot memo
artifact.survives_author: The JSON brief, evidence matrix, and report can be rerun by a new reviewer.
artifact.acceptance_criteria: A reviewer can trace every scored claim to its source tier and decision consequence.

feedback.next_move_threshold: Stop if one critical control failure occurs or measured rework reduction is below 10%.
feedback.review_question: Which missing evidence would most likely reverse the pilot decision?

alternatives: Do not start a pilot; continue manual review
decision_criteria: Measured rework reduction without critical control failure
Ability to stop and roll back within one business day
decision_review.reversibility: The pilot is time-limited, uses a restricted document set, and can return to manual review.
decision_review.key_cost_or_tradeoff: Human review and logging reduce automation speed but preserve accountability.
decision_review.premortem_failure: The pilot appears efficient while silently normalizing unsupported or unauthorized recommendations.

gates.concept: A pilot means a time-limited, restricted-document evaluation; it does not mean production deployment.
gates.evidence: Synthetic operational and evaluation claims are separated from public-framework claims and their limitations are explicit.
gates.action: The council can approve only a controlled pilot with named owners, stop conditions, and a manual fallback.

chain.claim: A controlled pilot is the strongest action currently supported.
chain.evidence: The synthetic baseline reports rework and the synthetic prototype reports partial accuracy, while both remain unvalidated on an unseen set.
chain.inference: The evidence supports learning under constraints but not broad deployment.
chain.action: Run a restricted human-in-the-loop pilot only after unseen-case and control tests pass.
chain.boundary: No autonomous procurement decision, supplier sanction, or supplier-facing action is permitted.
chain.counterevidence: Accuracy below 50% on an unseen set or one critical disclosure or agency failure blocks the pilot.

chain.claim: Risk review must cover governance and operational controls, not accuracy alone.
chain.evidence: NIST AI RMF and OWASP GenAI sources identify broader risk-management and security concerns.
chain.inference: A locally accountable control map and test plan are needed before the council can act.
chain.action: Map each pilot risk to an owner, test, evidence artifact, and stop condition.
chain.boundary: The cited frameworks inform the review but do not validate this local workflow or prove control effectiveness.
chain.counterevidence: If the council cannot map risks to named controls and accountable owners, postpone the pilot.

passes.name: judgment
passes.finding: The evidence supports a bounded learning decision, not a production-readiness claim.
passes.revision: Narrowed the recommendation to a controlled pilot and stated the default outcome.

passes.name: evidence
passes.finding: Synthetic results and public-framework guidance could be confused as equivalent proof.
passes.revision: Separated source tiers, human-check declarations, weakest links, and decision-changing gaps.

passes.name: structure
passes.finding: The decision chain needed an explicit bridge from evidence to action.
passes.revision: Added two claim-evidence-inference-action chains with boundaries and counterevidence.

passes.name: clarity
passes.finding: Pilot, approval, and deployment required sharper meanings.
passes.revision: Defined pilot as restricted and time-limited and excluded autonomous supplier-facing action.

passes.name: delivery
passes.finding: The packet needed to survive the author and support a council review.
passes.revision: Named the artifact owner, acceptance criteria, feedback channel, check-in date, and stop threshold.

boundaries: Fictional case; scope is limited to a controlled document-review pilot with named human approvers.
out_of_scope: Autonomous procurement decisions, legal conclusions, supplier sanctions, and broad production deployment are out of scope.
